key_insight: The paper builds lower-regularity finite element elasticity complexes on tetrahedral Alfeld splits, culminating in hybridizable symmetric stress spaces with fewer degrees of freedom and no vertex unknowns.
engineering_relevance: This enables more efficient, high-order mixed finite elements for 3D elasticity and incompatibility problems, with potential benefits for large-scale structural simulation.
method: Use local Bernstein-Gelfand-Gelfand constructions on polynomial de Rham complexes over Alfeld splits.
Derive two exact polynomial elasticity complexes: an H2–H1(inc) complex and a lower-regularity H1(curl)–H(inc+) complex.
Prove bubble subcomplexes, dimension formulas, unisolvent elements, and commuting interpolation for global finite element subcomplexes.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports two proved exact polynomial complexes, derived dimension formulas, unisolvent finite elements, and commuting interpolation diagrams, with the lowest-order stress tensor space being piecewise cubic.
watch_item: The elements are tailored to tetrahedral Alfeld splits and specialized differential-complex machinery, so practical implementation may be nontrivial compared with standard elasticity elements.
paper_title: Low-regularity finite element elasticity complexes with hybridizable stresses on tetrahedral Alfeld splits
